nyheter.title: Bollinger Quietly Cut Steel for First Arctic Security Cutter in April Ahead of $2.2B Award
nyheter.summary: The network of Bollinger shipyards in Louisiana wasted no time starting work on their Coast Arctic Security Cutter, beginning fabrication of the first planned U.S. hull two months ahead of the finalized contract, Bollinger president Ben Bordelon told USNI News this week. nyheter.title: Australia announces defense industry policy and acquisitions reforms
nyheter.summary: Australian Defence Minister Richard Marles said the reforms seek to build “a more agile, disciplined and strategically focused Defence organisation.”

nyheter.title: US Navy seeks to boost production of new anti-radar missile
nyheter.summary: Naval Air Systems Command is asking industry whether it is capable of supplying up to 600 Advanced Emission Suppression Missiles per year.

nyheter.summary:
On 2 July, PT PAL Indonesia and Naval Group organised a press tour of PT PAL’s submarine construction and maintenance facilities in Surabaya, East Java, to showcase preparations for the start of construction of two Scorpène Evolved submarines for the Indonesian Navy (TNI AL) under the Scorpène Republik Indonesia (SRI) programme. nyheter.summary:
At Eurosatory 2026, MBDA unveiled its new generation “Land Cruise Missile – Naval Cruise Missile (LCM – NCM) MK2”, the European very long-range and high-performance cruise missile. nyheter.title: HII Begins Fabrication of Flight III Destroyer John F. Lehman (DDG 137)
nyheter.summary:
HII’s Ingalls Shipbuilding division began fabrication of the future USS John F. Lehman (DDG 137) Monday, marking the official start of construction on the Navy’s newest Flight III Arleigh Burke‑class destroyer. nyheter.title: Sailor Missing After Helo Assigned to USS George H.W. Bush Ditches in Arabian Sea
nyheter.summary: One aircrewman is missing after an MH-60S helicopter assigned to USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) made an emergency landing on Wednesday in the Arabian Sea. The helicopter went down at 3:30 a.m. Eastern Time with four crew members on board, the U.S. 5th Fleet said on social media platform X. Three of the crew members were recovered from the water and are in stable condition aboard George H. W. Bush. U.S. Navy assets in the region are currently searching for the missing crew member. “There is no ind

nyheter.title: Denmark Orders New Multi-Role Environmental and Minelaying Vessels
nyheter.summary:
The Danish Ministry of Defence Acquisition and Logistics Organisation (DALO) announced that a contract for new marine environment and minelaying vessels for the Royal Danish Navy was signed on 4 February. The vessels will be capable of laying mines and supporting the Navy in missions such as maritime surveillance and the assertion of sovereignty. Denmark ...

nyheter.summary:
The UK’s new Defence Investment Plan (DIP) has detailed four platform types to be introduced into Royal Navy (RN) service, as central elements of the RN’s new ‘hybrid’ force structure and the spine of its ‘Atlantic Fleet’ operational concept series. nyheter.summary:
PENGUIN Shipyard International has completed and delivered a composite superstructure (CSS) using Saab’s unique technology for the Republic of Singapore Navy’s new Multi Role Combat Vessel (MRCV). nyheter.title: UK’s Starmer unveils $20 billion defense boost in long-delayed investment plan
nyheter.summary: The Defence Investment Plan falls short of the £28 billion($37 billion) wanted by defense chiefs and represents a 5% increase in annual defense spending.

nyheter.title: Russia, China Fly Joint Bomber Missions Near Japan, South Korea
nyheter.summary: Russia and China conducted joint bomber patrol flights on Saturday for the first time this year, prompting Japan and South Korea to scramble fighter aircraft. Two Chinese H-6 bombers flew from the East China Sea into the Sea of Japan where they were joined by two Russian Tu-95 bombers and two Russian Tu-142 Maritime Patrol Aircraft, according to Japan’s Joint Staff Office. The Russian and Chinese aircraft then conducted a joint flight back to the East China Sea. Two Chinese J-16 fighters and one

nyheter.title: Drones, fighters, armored vehicles: Highlights from the UK’s Defence Investment Plan
nyheter.summary: Beyond top line spending, one of the leading takeaways from the DIP is the clear push to acquire and field autonomous systems, underpinned by a £5 billion investment in the technology, which includes plans for a “hybrid navy.”

nyheter.title: U.S. Marines Use Commercial Ferries to Haul Anti-ship Missiles Across the Philippines
nyheter.summary: MANILA, PHILIPPINES ― Marine anti-ship missile launchers and air defense rolled off contracted Philippine commercial transport vessels onto an island in the middle of the Luzon Strait near Taiwan during drills earlier this month. nyheter.title: World War II Wreck Hunter RV Petrel Reborn as Navy Survey Ship Sarah Lynn
nyheter.summary: A billionaire’s ship designed to find the most famous lost ships of World War II has reemerged as a U.S. Navy survey ship, USNI News has learned. Service officials confirmed to USNI News that MV Sarah Lynn, which earlier this year operated off the coast of Alaska, is the former RV Petrel, the late Paul Allen-owned research vessel that found such famous World War II ships like USS Indianapolis (CA-35), USS Juneau (CL-52), Japanese carrier IJN Kaga, carriers USS Lexington (CV-2), USS Wasp (CV-7) a

nyheter.title: Marine Missing from USS Anchorage Declared Lost at Sea
nyheter.summary: A 21 year-old Marine from Minnesota died after going missing at sea off the California coast, the service announced Monday. Lance Cpl. Armando Ortiz Canseco was attached to San Antonio-class amphibious transport dock USS Anchorage (LPD-23) when he went missing on Thursday, according to a Marine Corps news release. The incident is still under investigation. “On behalf of the Marines and sailors of the 13th Marine Expeditionary Unit, I extend our deepest condolences to the family and loved ones of

nyheter.title: Former U.S. Amphib Sunk in Pacific Maritime Strike Drills
nyheter.summary: Decommissioned amphibious transport dock USS Juneau (LPD-10) was sunk by the U.S. and partner nations on Saturday in the Philippine Sea during the ongoing Valiant Shield 2026 exercise. Juneau was sunk more than 200 nautical miles off the coast in the Mariana Islands Range Complex during a sinking exercise that brought together air, surface and subsurface assets for coordinated strikes. nyheter.title: France Seizes 5th Russian Shadow Fleet Tanker
nyheter.summary: The French Navy boarded and diverted a suspected Russian shadow fleet tanker in the Mediterranean off Sicily, officials announced on Thursday. In a social media post on Thursday, French President Emmanuel Macron stated that the French Navy intercepted tanker MT Deliver on Tuesday as it transited off the coast of Sicily in violation of maritime law The seizure on Tuesday marked France’s fifth seizure of a suspected shadow fleet tanker since September 2025. rov_teknologi.summary: The U.S. and partner nations on Wednesday wrapped up Exercise Valiant Shield 2026, which involved 10 days of training that saw anti-submarine warfare, cutting edge drone testing and a multinational maritime strike. “What we promised in preparation, we proved in execution,” said Rear Adm. rov_teknologi.title: USS San Antonio Leaves Norfolk for Deployment
rov_teknologi.summary: Amphibious warship USS San Antonio (LPD-17) departed Naval Station Norfolk, Va., on Monday as part of an extended deployment after a brief maintenance period, USNI News has learned. A U.S. 2nd Fleet spokesperson confirmed San Antonio left Norfolk for a deployment but did not provide additional details on the mission. San Antonio returned in April from a 258-day deployment as part of the Iwo Jima Amphibious Ready Group mission to U.S. Southern Command. San Antonio deployed on Aug. 14, along with

rov_teknologi.summary:
Surabaya – On June 30, Indonesia’s state-owned shipbuilder PT PAL Indonesia launched the third Tarlac-class Landing Platform Dock (LPD) it built for the Philippine Navy. This is the first “improved” ship from the follow-on two-ship contract ordered by the Philippines in 2022, with the fourth LPD also expected to be launched later this year. The ...

rov_teknologi.summary:

rov_teknologi.summary: MANILA, PHILIPPINES – The latest tranche of American-made and funded drones were formally received by Philippine forces this week ahead of Washington’s larger plans to equip the Southeast Asian ally with asymmetric capabilities that could prove crucial in monitoring and deterring Beijing in the South China Sea.
